yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday delivered a perfect taper protocol: easy open water swim (1.3mi, HR 117) for 26 TSS, followed by a light course preview ride on ROUVY simulating the Oregon 70.3 route (18.2mi, HR 113) for just 29 TSS. Combined 55 TSS kept stress minimal while maintaining race-specific feel and familiarity with the course terrain—ideal execution 9 days out.
todayRecommendation: Complete rest as planned. No training stress today. This is your final zero day before race week begins—protect the taper, let adaptations solidify, and prioritize sleep quality heading into tomorrow's open water swim at La Jolla Shores.
weekProgress: Week 12 of 13: Currently at 179 of 200 TSS target (90% complete) with 2 days remaining. This is right on schedule—tomorrow's open water swim (1,500m) and Sunday's easy 2-3mi tune-up run will close the gap perfectly. Taper volume reduction (-60%) is being executed as designed with bike packed Thursday for Sunday's drive to Salem.
keyInsight: Nine days from race day with taper dialed in perfectly—training stress controlled, course preview completed, and recovery metrics strong with last night's 85 sleep score and 8.0h duration setting you up ideally for race week.
positives: Sleep rebounding strongly: 85 score with 8.0h duration and RHR 53 last night after mid-week dip
